refactor: rename survey-level axes (core→foundation, diff→progress+gap)

Clearer mapping from paper-level annotations to survey-level synthesis:
- core assumptions → foundation (shared technical building blocks)
- diff → progress (trajectory of advances) + gap (structural unsolved problems)
- Add aggregation logic explanation in SKILL.md

commit.message:
Add constituency & region maps, electricity/gas split, and new policy analyses

- Add constituency-level energy shock analysis (650 constituencies) using
  parliamentary constituency weights from policyengine-uk-data
- Add interactive D3 constituency map with 10 switchable metrics (burden,
  extra cost, fuel poverty under each shock scenario)
- Add regional energy map to the Electricity vs Gas section (By region tab)
- Add extended analysis (analysis_v2.py): electricity/gas split, regional/tenure
  breakdowns, NEF National Energy Guarantee, rising block tariff, gas-only cap
- Update Dashboard with EnergySplitSection, NewPoliciesSection, and map sections
- Include UK constituencies GeoJSON and lookup CSV

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Seerr API update, search performance fix, discovery sort/filter, collection discovery (#427)

* perf: speed up discovery and search by ~6-8x with caching

Backend:
- Cache Jellyfin→Jellyseerr user ID mapping (30min TTL), avoiding
  a full user list fetch on every proxy request
- Add per-user server-side response caching (10min TTL) for
  discovery, search, similar, and recommendation endpoints
- Reduce logging noise for high-frequency discovery endpoints

Frontend:
- Increase request manager cache TTL from 5min to 30min
- Double concurrency limit from 4 to 8 concurrent requests
- Reduce search debounce from 1000ms to 300ms
- Render search results immediately, enrich collections in background
- Replace polling-based DOM injection with MutationObserver
- Parallelize TMDB genre fetch with genre info and user status
- Prefetch TMDB genres on Jellyseerr initialization

Benchmarks (warm requests):
- JE Search: ~17ms → ~2-3ms
- Genre Discovery: ~15ms → ~2-3ms
- Network Discovery: ~15ms → ~2-3ms
- UI search perceived delay: ~1.3-6s → ~300-350ms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: comprehensive Jellyseerr API improvements (16 changes)

Bug fixes:
- Return jellyseerrUserId from /user-status (eliminates /user?take=1000 from frontend)
- Remove misplaced [Authorize] on private IsCacheableApiPath method
- Remove dead /tv/{tmdbId}/seasons endpoint (was proxying to 404)

Performance (backend):
- Combine user-status into single network call (skip /status when user found)
- Add 15s HttpClient timeout on all Jellyseerr-bound clients
- Cache /person/ and /collection/ responses server-side
- Fix cache eviction to run every 50 writes, not only when >200 entries

Performance (frontend):
- fetchMovieCollection: use Jellyseerr movie detail first, TMDB fallback
- fetchAdvancedRequestData: parallel Promise.all for server details
- Cache override rules for 5 minutes

Code quality:
- Extract fetchRelated() helper replacing 4 near-identical functions
- Fix addCollections immutability (returns new array, no mutation)
- Route POST requests through request manager for concurrency control

New capabilities:
- Search passes language param from navigator.language
- All 6 discover endpoints forward sortBy/dates/ratings/runtime/cert/watchRegion
- New genreslider/movie and genreslider/tv endpoints + API function

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add sort dropdown and genre slider backdrop cards to discovery UI

Sort dropdown (Popular/Top Rated/Newest/Oldest) on all discovery sections
(genre, network/studio, tag). Sort selection clears and re-fetches results
with the sortBy query param. Genre slider renders a horizontal scrollable
row of TMDB genre cards with backdrop images at the top of genre discovery.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: TV sort uses first_air_date instead of release_date, remove genre slider

TMDB's discover/tv endpoint uses first_air_date not release_date for date
sorting. Added getTvSortMode() helper that translates release_date to
first_air_date for all TV fetch functions. Removed genre slider UI for now.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add sort to person discovery, fix network/studio sort support

- Person discovery: client-side sort (Popular/Top Rated/Newest/Oldest)
  since combined_credits is a non-paginated endpoint
- Network/studio: switched from specific Jellyseerr endpoints
  (/discover/tv/network/X, /discover/movies/studio/X) to generic
  discover endpoints (/discover/tv?network=X, /discover/movies?studio=X)
  which support sortBy and other filter params

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: code review fixes — cache mutation, race conditions, JSDoc

- api.search: return immutable copy instead of mutating cached response
- api.requestMedia: build body immutably with spread instead of mutation
- loadMoreItems (all 3 modules): move isLoading=false to finally block
  so it always resets even on re-thrown errors; roll back page counters
  on fetch failure so retry doesn't skip pages
- Add JSDoc to all new/modified functions per CONTRIBUTING.md:
  getSortMode, getTvSortMode, setSortMode, resetSortMode,
  createSectionContainer (updated params), handleSortChange,
  getPagedResultsForMode, renderChunk, loadMoreItems
- Add inline comments for complex logic blocks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add infinite scroll to search results

Search results now load continuously as you scroll down instead of
showing only the first page. Layout changed from horizontal scroller
to vertical grid matching discovery pages.

- Add page param to C# search endpoint and JS api.search()
- Add pagination state, loadMoreSearchResults, and deduplication
- Wire up seamlessScroll with prefetch, retry, and IntersectionObserver
- Switch search results to vertical-wrap grid layout
- Reset pagination on query change and manual refresh

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add admin toggle to disable server-side response cache

Adds a "Disable server-side response cache" checkbox under a new
Testing / Debug section in the Jellyseerr admin config panel. When
enabled, all proxy requests bypass both the response cache and the
user ID cache, hitting Jellyseerr directly every time.

- Add JellyseerrDisableCache to PluginConfiguration
- Gate response cache and user ID cache on the setting
- Expose setting in public-config endpoint
- Add checkbox with description in configPage.html

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add configurable cache TTL to admin panel

Response cache TTL (default 10 min) and user ID cache TTL (default
30 min) are now configurable in the Testing / Debug section of the
Jellyseerr admin panel. Values are clamped to a minimum of 1 minute.

- Add JellyseerrResponseCacheTtlMinutes and JellyseerrUserIdCacheTtlMinutes
  to PluginConfiguration
- Replace hardcoded TimeSpan constants with GetResponseCacheTtl() and
  GetUserIdCacheTtl() helpers that read from config
- Add number inputs to configPage.html with load/save wiring

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: left-align search results grid to match Jellyfin layout

Remove centered class from Jellyseerr search results container so
cards align with the rest of the Jellyfin card grid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: rename Jellyseerr to Seerr in comments and log strings

Update all comments, JSDoc descriptions, log prefixes, and UI
description text to use "Seerr" instead of "Jellyseerr" following
the upstream name change. Identifiers, config keys, CSS classes,
and DOM selectors are left unchanged for backwards compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add JSDoc annotations per CONTRIBUTING.md guidelines

Add @param/@returns tags to all public functions across discovery
modules and api.js. Remove duplicate JSDoc block in person-discovery.js.
Fix console.log to console.debug with logPrefix in jellyseerr.js.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add collection discovery + code review fixes

- Add collection discovery: shows missing movies on BoxSet detail pages

- Add /boxset/{id} API endpoint with type validation
- Add config toggle for collection discovery
- Fix sort race condition in genre/network/tag discovery (abort previous

- Fix BoxSet endpoint to validate item is actually a BoxSet type
- Fix request body logged at INFO level (now Debug)
- Fix JSDoc @param name mismatch in ui.js after Seerr rename
- Add metrics tracking to collection discovery

commit.message:
feat: runtime input automation — bridge-backed input injection and recording

Route all input simulation commands (simulate_action, simulate_key,
simulate_mouse_click, simulate_mouse_move, simulate_sequence) through
the runtime bridge when the game is running. Falls back to editor-side
injection when no bridge is connected. Both paths support duration-based
press-and-release. Responses include target field (runtime/editor).

Recording now happens game-side via _input() hook, capturing key, mouse,
joypad, and action events with timestamps. Action injection records
through _maybe_record() helper that enforces bounds uniformly. Replay
dispatches events with proper timing in the game process.

Removed ui_* filter from action suggestions. Removed experimental
labels from recording commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ERTMS Mode Explorer — interactive ETCS state diagram and simulator

Interactive web app for exploring ERTMS/ETCS operating modes (FS, SR, SB, TR, etc.)
and transitions between them. Features:
- SVG state diagram with pan/zoom, hover highlighting, click for details
- Incoming/outgoing arrow color differentiation (blue/green)
- Direction filter (outgoing/incoming/both) to reduce visual clutter
- Category filters and common-only toggle
- Detail panel with plain-language + technical descriptions
- Scenario simulator with guided quizzes and free exploration
- 16 ETCS modes, 50+ transitions from Subset-026

Built with React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS v4, Framer Motion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add AI-powered definition drafting for data catalog

Enables stewards and admins to draft field definitions using configurable
AI models (OpenAI or Anthropic). The AI searches ServiceNow documentation
first, then falls back to training knowledge. Drafts are returned for
review before saving, preserving the existing review workflow.

- Add draft-definition API endpoint with ServiceNow docs search

- Track AI source metadata (model name, ID) on saved definitions
- Add AI generated filter option to catalog source dropdown

Enhance SEO Person schema with structured biography fields (#561)

* Add design doc for SEO Person schema enhancement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add SEO Person schema implementation plan

10-task TDD implementation plan for enhancing schema.org Person markup
with fields extracted during biography enrichment (alternate names,
gender, nationality, occupations, awards).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add biography SEO fields migration (alternate_names, gender, nationality, occupations, awards)

Add occupations column and GIN index that were missing from the original
migration run. The migration file now includes all 5 columns on
actor_biography_details (alternate_names, gender, nationality, occupations,
awards), the denormalized alternate_names on actors, and a GIN index for
array search.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add SEO fields to BiographyData type and Claude synthesis prompt

Add alternateNames, gender, nationality, occupations, and awards to the
BiographyData interface and Claude synthesis prompt. These structured fields
enable richer schema.org Person markup without polluting the narrative text.

Key changes:
- Add 5 new fields to BiographyData interface in types.ts
- Add field descriptions to Claude prompt JSON schema
- Update CRITICAL section to redirect awards from narrative to structured field
- Parse new fields from Claude response with safe defaults
- Update test helpers to include new required fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Write SEO fields to actor_biography_details and denormalize alternate_names

Add 5 new columns (alternate_names, gender, nationality, occupations,
awards) to the INSERT/UPSERT in the biography DB writer, following the
existing COALESCE pattern so re-enrichment preserves non-null values.
Empty arrays are converted to null for COALESCE to work correctly.

Also denormalizes alternate_names to the actors table for search, only
when the array is non-empty.

Updates tests to verify new parameter positions, SEO field values,
and alternate_names denormalization behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add P742 (pseudonym) to Wikidata biography SPARQL query

Adds Wikidata property P742 (pseudonym) to the biography SPARQL query
so alternate names / stage names from Wikidata feed into Claude
synthesis alongside web-sourced alternate names.

Changes:
- Add P742 OPTIONAL pattern to SPARQL query (string literal, no label join needed)
- Add pseudonyms to WikidataBioBinding and ParsedWikidataBio interfaces
- Parse pseudonyms in parseResults using filterValidLabels
- Format as "Also known as:" line in formatBiographyText
- Include pseudonyms in confidence calculation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Enhance Person schema with SEO fields (alternateName, gender, nationality, occupation, award)

Update both client-side and server-side buildPersonSchema to emit richer
structured data for SEO. Add DEPARTMENT_TO_TITLE mapping to derive jobTitle
from known_for_department field instead of hardcoding "Actor".

New optional fields: alternateNames, gender, nationality, occupations,
awards, education, knownForDepartment. All fields are omitted from
JSON-LD output when not provided (undefined).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Pass SEO fields through actor route, frontend types, and schema builders

- Add alternate_names, gender, nationality, occupations, awards to actor
  route SQL query and response mapping
- Update frontend BiographyDetails type with new SEO fields
- Pass new fields from biographyDetails to buildPersonSchema in ActorPage
- Query biography details SEO fields in prerender data-fetcher for
  server-side schema generation
- Update test mock data to include new required BiographyDetails fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Search actors by alternate names

Add alternate_names::text ILIKE matching to the actor search SQL query
so users can find actors by birth names or other alternate names (e.g.,
searching "Marion Morrison" finds John Wayne).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove BiographyLifeDetails component (structured fields used for SEO only)

The BiographyLifeDetails component rendered structured biography fields
(birthplace, family, education, etc.) below the narrative text. These
fields duplicate the narrative content and do not add standalone value
as visible UI text. They are now used exclusively for SEO via the
schema.org Person markup in the page head.

Changes:
- Remove BiographyLifeDetails import and rendering from BiographySection
- Delete BiographyLifeDetails.tsx and BiographyLifeDetails.test.tsx
- Remove corresponding tests from BiographySection.test.tsx

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump BIO_ENRICHMENT_VERSION to 6.0.0 and update documentation

Version bump enables the resynthesize-biographies script to target actors
with version < 6.0.0 for re-synthesis with the new SEO fields (alternateNames,
gender, nationality, occupations, awards).

- Bump BIO_ENRICHMENT_VERSION from 5.1.0 to 6.0.0
- Update test assertions in db-writer and integration tests
- Add new fields to Output Fields table in docs/biography-system.md
- Add new fields to BiographyData Fields table in biography-enrichment.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix hasOccupation schema type from Role to Occupation

schema.org/hasOccupation expects Occupation type with name property,
not Role with roleName. Updated both client and server schema builders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Wire known_for_department through full stack for dynamic jobTitle schema

The schema.org Person markup was hardcoding jobTitle as Actor despite
having DEPARTMENT_TO_TITLE mapping. This adds known_for_department to
the actors table and wires it through TMDB ingest, API responses, and
frontend schema builders so jobTitle dynamically reflects the actual
department (Acting->Actor, Directing->Director, Writing->Writer).

Changes:
- Add known_for_department column to actors table (migration)
- Add to ActorRecord type, TMDBPerson, TMDBAggregateActor interfaces
- Wire through upsertActor/batchUpsertActors with COALESCE preservation
- Pass through movie, show, and sync-tmdb-people ingest paths
- Add knownForDepartment to actor API response and frontend types
- Fix integration test parameter positions for new SEO fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix review issues: typed schema.org objects, missing type field, SEO test coverage

Three issues found during branch review:

1. Add alternate_names to ActorRecord type (matches migration column)
2. Wrap alumniOf and nationality with typed schema.org objects
   (EducationalOrganization and Country) in both client and server
   schema builders
3. Add SEO field test coverage to claude-cleanup.test.ts: field parsing,
   "null" string handling, awards cap at 5, non-string filtering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address Copilot review feedback

- Fix test name: "Role array" → "Occupation array" to match schema.org type
- Always sync alternate_names to actors table (set null when empty to
  prevent stale data from previous enrichment runs)
- Add cross-reference comments for DEPARTMENT_TO_TITLE duplication between
  client and server schema builders

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add drag-and-drop email builder (Unlayer) to campaigns and templates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct HMAC verification in send-email auth hook (#132)

GoTrue signs hook requests using raw UTF-8 bytes of the full secret
string as the HMAC-SHA256 key. The previous implementation incorrectly
used atob() to base64-decode the secret before importing it, causing
every verification attempt to fail with 401/500 errors on signup.

Also documents SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY in .env.example.

commit.message:
fix: reset stale working states on startup + 24h inbox TTL

On startup, reset all "working" agent states to idle — handles
orphaned containers from previous session (process.exit() bypasses
container close handlers, leaving states stuck at "working").

Also add 24h TTL to inbox messages — expired messages are discarded
on drain to prevent stale triggers causing wrong agent behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add team-aware cutscenes: real names, race/class art, state-reactive tone

Cutscene engine now uses actual game state when generating procedural
cutscenes — real team member names in dialogue, character art matching
team race/class, tone weighting based on HP/fallen/veteran status, and
state-reactive bonus frames (solo hero, critical HP, fallen allies,
veteran, legendary). Fully backward compatible when no context passed.

Hearth: move Events panel into right column below Live Activity (Forge-hco) (#87)

* feat: move Events panel into right column below Live Activity

Restructure the Hearth TUI layout so Events sits in the right column
below Live Activity, mirroring how Needs Attention sits below Queue
in the left column. The layout is now symmetrical:
  Left  = Queue + Needs Attention
  Center = Workers (full height)
  Right  = Live Activity + Events

The bottom full-width event strip is removed. A shared
renderStackedColumn helper handles the two-panel stacking for both
left and right columns.

Bead: Forge-hco

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-hco

- Clarify renderStackedColumn comment to explain why bottomHeight is
  reduced by 2: each lipgloss panel adds 2 border lines, so two stacked
  panels produce 4 border lines vs 2 for the single center column; the
  -2 adjustment keeps total rendered heights aligned.
- Add minimum bottom panel height (4 lines) in getVerticalSplit to
  prevent bordered panels from vanishing at small terminal sizes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use JavaScriptBridge.fetch for leaderboard on web builds

Godot HTTPRequest with custom headers has issues in some browsers (Safari/iOS).
Web build now uses native JS fetch() via JavaScriptBridge for reliable CORS handling.
Desktop build keeps existing HTTPRequest path unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: DM scroll-to-bottom + modal bleed-through

DM scroll fix:
- Root cause: when last server channel was a forum, the scroll container
  (ref={msgScrollRef}) was hidden by {activeCh?.type!=='forum'} condition,
  making scrollToBottom() a no-op (msgScrollRef.current === null)
- Fix: gate forum view on activeView==='servers', messages div always renders for DMs
- Also include activeView in scroll-reset effect and useLayoutEffect deps
  so returning to an already-loaded DM also scrolls to bottom

Modal bleed-through fix:
- Added glass-modal CSS class: rgba(11,11,20,0.96) — 96% opacity eliminates
  background content bleeding through profile, settings, etc. modals
- gm constant now uses glass-modal instead of glass-panel
- glass-panel bumped to 0.82 for sidebars/nav
- glass-dark bumped to 0.60 for chat area background

commit.message:
feat: implement Phase 2 — daemon, indexer pipeline, and CLI

- Background daemon with PID management and Unix socket IPC
- IndexerService: parse → resolve → embed → upsert pipeline
- Tree-sitter TypeScript/JS parser with entity and relation extraction
- @parcel/watcher file watching with debounce and ignore patterns
- Ollama embedding integration (qwen3-embedding:0.6b, batch 16)
- Manifest parser for package.json, go.mod, pyproject.toml, requirements.txt
- Relative import resolver with TS extension remapping
- IndexQueue with deduplication and async drain loop
- CLI commands: daemon start/stop/status, repo add/remove/list

commit.message:
Fix syntax error: escape literal newlines in hint strings

Literal newline chars inside JS string literals = syntax error.
lens.js failed to parse entirely, breaking the listen tap.
Replace 0x0a bytes in hint values with the \n escape sequence.

commit.message:
[Parity] OnboardingFlow QR scanning and permission management (#238)

* Implement QR scanning and comprehensive permission management in onboarding

Co-authored-by: yuga-hashimoto <74749461+yuga-hashimoto@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review comments and add japanese translations

* fix: add missing translations for all supported languages

* fix: resolve CI build and lint errors (fr string escape, missing string)

* Implement QR scanning and comprehensive permission management in onboarding

- Add QR scanning support using journeyapps barcode scanner
- Add PermissionToggle and SpecialAccessToggle enums for 10 permissions and 2 special access modes
- Implement hardware capability detection for SMS and Motion
- Port official onboarding design tokens (gradients, colors, typography)
- Add missing string resources to all localized strings.xml files
- Fix pre-existing invalid escape sequences in Chinese translation

Co-authored-by: yuga-hashimoto <74749461+yuga-hashimoto@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix lint error on qr_scan_prompt and address PR review comments

* Implement QR scanning and comprehensive permission management in onboarding

- Add QR scanning support using journeyapps barcode scanner
- Add PermissionToggle and SpecialAccessToggle enums for 10 permissions and 2 special access modes
- Implement hardware capability detection for SMS and Motion
- Port official onboarding design tokens (gradients, colors, typography)
- Add missing string resources to all localized strings.xml files
- Fix positional parameter issues in localized strings and sync them with base strings
- Fix pre-existing invalid escape sequences in Chinese translation
- Added Scan QR strings to base strings.xml to resolve lint errors

Co-authored-by: yuga-hashimoto <74749461+yuga-hashimoto@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: setup code supports both QR scan and text input with dark-bg visibility fix + i18n for all 8 languages

* fix: pin Next button at bottom of all setup steps, fix strings.xml conflicts/duplicates

* fix: fallback to system sans-serif font to resolve startup crash caused by missing Manrope TTF

* fix: resolve crash on PermissionsStep by using correct string format arguments (%1$d instead of %1)

* fix: add missing 'none' and 'capability_motion' strings to default and ja locale

Resolves lint ExtraTranslation error - these keys existed in de/es/fr/hi/ru/zh-rCN/zh-rTW
but were absent from values/strings.xml (default) and values-ja/strings.xml.

commit.message:
feat: geometry conditioning for FLUX 3D panels + multi-variant DNA (#41)

* fix: add FLUX.1-schnell automatic fallback when FLUX.1-dev is down

When FLUX.1-dev returns persistent 500/503 errors (server outage),
automatically fall back to FLUX.1-schnell (4 steps) which uses a
different endpoint and may still be online. This prevents complete
generation failure during FLUX.1-dev outages.

Fallback chain: FLUX.1-dev (5 retries) → without init_image → FLUX.1-schnell → fail

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: enable geometry conditioning for FLUX 3D panels and add multi-variant DNA

- Enable canonical geometry pack init_image for hero_3d, axonometric, interior_3d
  (server-side SVG→PNG rasterization in api/together-image.js handles conversion)
- Tune STRENGTH_POLICY for photorealistic output: hero_3d 0.30 (70% creative
  freedom), axonometric 0.25, interior_3d 0.35
- Add generateDNAVariants() to TwoPassDNAGenerator: generates 3 parallel DNA
  variants (Conservative temp=0.1, Moderate temp=0.3, Bold temp=0.6) for design
  exploration
- Make passA temperature and style hint configurable via options parameter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
